TestName: Reject Voucher Creation When Voucher Amount Does Not Match Payable Amount
Preconditions: Operational items with calculated payable amounts exist
User is authorized to create vouchers
Steps: Construct a payment voucher payload with operational items and an incorrect voucher amount
Submit a POST request to /api/vouchers with the payload
Receive response and parse error details
Expected: Response indicates failure with an error message describing voucher amount mismatch and created=false in the response body

TestName: Ensure Voucher Traceability to Operational Items
Preconditions: At least one voucher exists with associated operational items
User is authorized to retrieve voucher information
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/vouchers/{voucherId} with a valid voucher ID
Parse the response to retrieve linked items, batches, or services
Expected: Response includes detailed references to all items, batches, or services that generated the payable amount, ensuring traceability

TestName: Voucher Traceability Missing Links Should Fail Validation
Preconditions: User is authorized to create vouchers
Steps: Construct a payment voucher payload without references to operational items, batches, or services
Submit a POST request to /api/vouchers with the incomplete payload
Receive and parse response
Expected: Response includes a validation error message indicating missing traceability data and created=false

TestName: Prevent creation of duplicate payment voucher for identical payable content
Preconditions: An existing payment voucher has been created for payable business content ID 12345
Steps: Send POST request to /api/vouchers with payload containing payable business content ID 12345
Attempt to create a second voucher with the same payable business content without partial or revised voucher flags
Expected: Response indicates failure with created=false and error.message 'Duplicate voucher not allowed for the same payable content'

TestName: Allow creation of revised payment voucher when flagged as intentional revision
Preconditions: An existing payment voucher has been created for payable business content ID 56789
Steps: Send POST request to /api/vouchers with payload containing payable business content ID 56789 and a flag indicating intentional revised voucher behavior
Expected: Response indicates voucher creation succeeded with created=true and a new voucher ID

TestName: Prevent double invoicing of identical billable content
Preconditions: An existing invoice has been generated for billable content ID 98765
Steps: Send POST request to /api/invoices with payload referencing billable content ID 98765
Attempt to create a second invoice for the same billable content without explicit allowance
Expected: Response includes created=false and error.message 'Duplicate billing not permitted for this content'

TestName: Allow duplicate billing when explicitly allowed by business model
Preconditions: Billable content ID 54321 is configured to allow duplicate billing
Steps: Send POST request to /api/invoices with payload containing billable content ID 54321
Send a second POST request to /api/invoices with the same billable content ID 54321
Expected: Both responses indicate created=true with unique invoice IDs

TestName: Modify Finalized Invoice Should Fail
Preconditions: An invoice exists with status 'finalized'
Steps: Send a PUT request to /invoices/{invoiceId} to modify the invoice details
Expected: The system rejects the modification and returns an error indicating the invoice is finalized and cannot be freely modified

TestName: Void Finalized Invoice Successfully
Preconditions: An invoice exists with status 'finalized'
Steps: Send a POST request to /invoices/{invoiceId}/void to void the finalized invoice
Expected: The invoice status changes to 'voided' and the response confirms successful voiding

TestName: Issue Replacement for Finalized Invoice
Preconditions: An invoice exists with status 'finalized' which needs amendment
Steps: Send a POST request to /invoices with replacement invoice details linking to the original finalized invoice
Expected: A replacement invoice record is created successfully and is linked to the original finalized invoice

TestName: Attempt Free Modification Without Void or Replacement
Preconditions: Invoice status is 'finalized'
Steps: Attempt to update invoice fields directly with PUT request to /invoices/{invoiceId}
Expected: Response body contains error message highlighting 'Invoice finalized - direct modifications are disallowed' and modification flags false

TestName: PreserveInvoiceRecordWhenVoided
Preconditions: An existing valid invoice with ID {invoiceId}
Steps: Send a PUT request to /api/invoices/{invoiceId}/void to void the invoice
Retrieve the invoice record using GET /api/invoices/{invoiceId}
Expected: The invoice record remains present with status marked as 'voided' and historical relationship data unchanged

TestName: InvoiceNotDeletedOnVoid
Preconditions: Invoice with ID {invoiceId} was voided
Steps: Send GET request to /api/invoices/{invoiceId}
Expected: Invoice record is returned with status indicating it is voided, not deleted or missing

TestName: PreventReceiptOnVoidedInvoice
Preconditions: Invoice with ID {invoiceId} is voided
Steps: Attempt to apply a receipt to the voided invoice by POSTing to /api/receipts/apply with invoiceId {invoiceId}
Expected: System rejects receipt application with error indicating the target invoice is voided and not receivable

TestName: AllowReceiptApplicationWhenBusinessRulePermits
Preconditions: Invoice with ID {invoiceId} is voided
Business rules explicitly permit receipt application to this voided invoice
Steps: Apply a receipt by POSTing to /api/receipts/apply with invoiceId {invoiceId}
Expected: Receipt application succeeds and confirmation is returned

TestName: RejectReceiptOnInvalidFinancialDocument
Preconditions: Financial document with ID {docId} is marked as invalid
Steps: Attempt receipt application to invalid document by POST to /api/receipts/apply with documentId {docId}
Expected: Receipt application is rejected with error indicating document is invalid and not receivable

TestName: Validate Profit and Loss Report with Accurate Revenue and Cost Data
Preconditions: Valid business records with accurate revenue and cost figures exist in the system
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/profit-loss/report with a valid date range and business identifiers
Retrieve the generated profit and loss report
Expected: The profit and loss report accurately reflects the revenue and cost figures derived from the valid business records.

TestName: Reject Profit and Loss Report Generation with Invalid Revenue Data
Preconditions: Business records exist but revenue data has inconsistencies or invalid entries
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/profit-loss/report with a valid date range and identifiers
System attempts to generate the profit and loss report
Expected: The response body includes an error flag indicating inaccurate revenue data and the report is not generated or contains created=false with a clear error message.

TestName: Reject Profit and Loss Report Generation with Missing Cost Figures
Preconditions: Business records exist but cost figures are missing or incomplete
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/profit-loss/report with a valid date range and business identifiers
System attempts to generate the profit and loss report
Expected: The system returns a response indicating failure to generate the report due to invalid or incomplete cost figures, with an error message specifying the data issue and created=false.

TestName: VerifyFinancialRecordDeletionIsPrevented
Preconditions: A financially relevant record exists with ID {recordId}
Steps: Send DELETE request to /financial-records/{recordId} to delete the financially relevant record
Expected: The deletion is rejected with a business-level error indicating preservation of historical data, and the record remains intact in the system

TestName: VerifyHistoricalReportIncludesDeletedRecordData
Preconditions: A financially relevant record existed and was marked as inactive or logically deleted but not physically removed
Steps: Send GET request to /historical-reports/{period}
Verify report content includes data from the previously marked inactive or logically deleted financial records
Expected: Historical report preserves prior events with no distortion caused by record deletion, reflecting accurate financial history

TestName: VerifyLogicalDeletionDoesNotChangeHistoricalReports
Preconditions: A financially relevant record exists with ID {recordId}
Steps: Send PUT request to /financial-records/{recordId} to logically delete the record (e.g., set active=false)
Request a historical report for the relevant period
Expected: Logical deletion allowed but historical reports remain unchanged and consistent with original business events

TestName: RejectPhysicalDeletionOfFinancialRecords
Preconditions: Financially relevant records exist
Steps: Send POST request to /financial-records/batch-delete with IDs of financially relevant records for deletion
Expected: Batch deletion request fails with a detailed error message preventing loss of historical business events

TestName: Identify valid uninvoiced business content successfully
Preconditions: At least one valid business content record exists and is not yet invoiced according to billing rules
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/v1/uninvoiced-content to fetch uninvoiced business content
Verify the response payload includes the valid uninvoiced business content matching billing rules
Expected: Response contains a list of valid uninvoiced business content items that comply with billing rules, with no content that has already been invoiced

TestName: Exclude invoiced business content from uninvoiced report
Preconditions: Business content records exist that have already been invoiced according to billing rules
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/v1/uninvoiced-content
Verify the response payload does not include any business content that has already been invoiced
Expected: Response does not contain any business content items that have been invoiced according to billing rules

TestName: Handle no uninvoiced business content present
Preconditions: All existing business content records are invoiced according to billing rules
Steps: Send a GET request to /api/v1/uninvoiced-content
Verify the response returns an empty list indicating no uninvoiced business content available
Expected: Response contains an empty array as no valid uninvoiced business content exists

TestName: Verify audit log entry created after invoice creation
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to create invoices
Steps: Send POST request to /api/invoices with valid invoice data
Retrieve audit logs for invoice creation events
Expected: A new audit log entry corresponding to the invoice creation is recorded with correct details including user, timestamp, and invoice ID

TestName: Verify audit log entry created after invoice voiding
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to void invoices
Invoice exists and is not already voided
Steps: Send POST request to /api/invoices/{invoiceId}/void to void an existing invoice
Retrieve audit logs for invoice voiding events
Expected: A new audit log entry is created recording the voiding action including user, timestamp, and invoice ID

TestName: Verify audit log entry created after receipt recording
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to record receipts
Steps: Send POST request to /api/receipts with valid receipt data
Retrieve audit logs for receipt recording events
Expected: Receipt recording action logged with an audit entry containing user, timestamp, and receipt details

TestName: Verify audit log entry created after voucher creation
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to create vouchers
Steps: Send POST request to /api/vouchers with valid voucher data
Retrieve audit logs for voucher creation events
Expected: Audit log contains a new entry documenting the voucher creation with relevant details

TestName: Reject audit actions if audit log system is unavailable during invoice creation
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized
Audit log service is down or unavailable
Steps: Send POST request to /api/invoices with valid invoice data while audit log service is unavailable
Expected: The invoice creation is rejected or rolled back with an error indicating audit log failure; no invoice record is created

TestName: Audit log entry contains all mandatory fields after receipt recording
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to record receipts
Steps: Send POST request to /api/receipts with valid receipt data
Fetch the latest audit log entry for receipt recording
Expected: Audit log entry contains user ID, action type, timestamp, receipt ID, and relevant metadata

TestName: Positive - Successful Request Passes Validation
Preconditions: API service is running
Valid authentication if required
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with all required valid fields in the payload
Validate that the request payload matches the API specification
Expected: The response should indicate success with no validation errors and return the expected data result

TestName: Negative - Missing Required Field Returns Clear Validation Error
Preconditions: API service is running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with a payload missing a required field
Observe the response from the server
Expected: The response contains a clear and meaningful error message specifying the missing required field; the error field 'error.message' describes the validation failure; the response indicates the request was rejected

TestName: Negative - Invalid Data Type Returns Clear Validation Feedback
Preconditions: API service is running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with a payload where a field has an invalid data type (e.g., string instead of integer)
Observe the response from the server
Expected: The response contains a clear error message identifying the data type violation; the error field 'error.message' explains the type mismatch and rejects the request

TestName: Negative - Invalid Field Format Returns Validation Error Message
Preconditions: API service is running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with a field that violates format constraints (e.g., an email field with an invalid email)
Observe the response
Expected: The response provides a clear validation error message specifying which field failed validation and why; error.message is descriptive about the format issue; request is rejected

TestName: Negative - Request With Extra Unknown Fields Returns Clear Validation Error
Preconditions: API service is running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource including additional fields not defined in the API schema
Observe the response
Expected: The response contains a clear error message indicating unknown or extraneous fields detected; the error message clearly identifies these fields and rejects the request

TestName: Negative - Empty Payload Returns Clear Validation Error Message
Preconditions: API service is running
Steps: Send a POST request to /api/resource with an empty payload
Observe the response
Expected: The response clearly states that the payload is missing or empty and rejects the request with an appropriate error message

TestName: Handle Validation Failure Consistently Across Modules
Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Steps: Send POST request to /api/module1/create with invalid payload missing required fields
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 400 with consistent error message structure { error: { message: 'Validation failed', fields: [...] } }

TestName: Handle Missing Reference Error Consistently in Module 2
Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Steps: Send PUT request to /api/module2/update with a reference ID that does not exist
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 404 with error message { error: { message: 'Reference not found' } } matching error format of other modules

TestName: Handle State Violation Consistently Across Modules
Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Entity state precondition that makes the state change invalid
Steps: Send POST request to /api/module3/changeState with invalid state transition parameters
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 409 with error message { error: { message: 'State violation: invalid state transition' } } formatted consistently with other error responses

TestName: Reject Requests with Missing Authentication Token
Steps: Send GET request to /api/module1/resource without any authentication token
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 401 with error message { error: { message: 'Unauthorized access: Token missing' } } consistent across modules

TestName: Reject Requests with Invalid Authentication Token
Steps: Send GET request to /api/module2/resource with expired or invalid authentication token
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 401 with error message { error: { message: 'Unauthorized access: Invalid token' } } matching error format of other modules

TestName: Reject Unauthorized Access to Protected Resource
Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token but without admin privileges
Steps: Send POST request to /api/module3/adminOnlyEndpoint with valid token but insufficient permissions
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 403 with error message { error: { message: 'Unauthorized access: Insufficient permissions' } } consistent with other modules

TestName: Accept Valid Request with Correct Data and Auth
Preconditions: User is authenticated with valid token
Steps: Send POST request to /api/module1/create with valid payload
Check response
Expected: Response status code is 201 with created=true and no error field, confirming successful processing

TestName: Prevent Update of A Record Invalidating Completed Downstream Without Reversal Logic
Preconditions: An A record exists with downstream processing completed
No reversal logic implemented for this record
Steps: Send a PUT request to /api/records/a/{id} to change fields impacting downstream data integrity
Verify the response indicates update is forbidden
Expected: The update is rejected with a business-level failure indicating that the update would invalidate completed downstream processing and no reversal logic exists

TestName: Allow Update of A Record When Reversal Logic Exists
Preconditions: An A record exists with downstream processing completed
Reversal logic is implemented for this record
Steps: Send a PUT request to /api/records/a/{id} to change fields impacting downstream data integrity
Verify the response indicates update was successful and reversal logic executed
Expected: The update is accepted and reversal logic is triggered to maintain downstream data consistency

TestName: Allow Update of A Record That Does Not Affect Completed Downstream Processing
Preconditions: An A record exists with downstream processing completed
Steps: Send a PUT request to /api/records/a/{id} to change non-dependent fields
Verify the response indicates update was successful
Expected: The update is accepted as it does not invalidate completed downstream processing

TestName: VerifyOperationalFinancialRecordsLinkageDuringTransactionCreation
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to create transactions
Steps: Send a POST request to /transactions with valid operational record and corresponding financial record details
Check the response for transaction creation confirmation and linkage identifiers
Expected: The system creates a business transaction where operational and financial records are correctly linked and persisted with consistent identifiers

TestName: FailTransactionCreationWhenOperationalRecordMissing
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to create transactions
Steps: Send a POST request to /transactions with missing operational record data but with financial record details
Observe the response for failure indication
Expected: The system rejects the transaction creation indicating operational record is required; response contains created=false and error.message

TestName: FailTransactionCreationWhenFinancialRecordMissing
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized to create transactions
Steps: Send a POST request to /transactions with operational record data but missing financial record data
Check response for rejection message
Expected: The system rejects the transaction creation because a linked financial record is required; response contains created=false and error.message

TestName: VerifyOperationalFinancialLinkageDuringTransactionUpdate
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized
Existing transaction with linked operational and financial records
Steps: Send a PUT request to /transactions/{transactionId} with updated operational or financial record details
Verify the linkage between operational and financial records is maintained after update
Expected: The system updates the transaction preserving the linkage integrity between operational and financial records

TestName: FailTransactionUpdateIfLinkBroken
Preconditions: User is authenticated and authorized
Existing transaction with linked records
Steps: Send a PUT request to /transactions/{transactionId} attempting to unlink or remove financial record linkage
Observe the response for failure due to linkage violation
Expected: The system rejects the update to prevent breaking the linkage between operational and financial records; response contains updated=false and error.message
